summary: Spec-doc embeddings should be chunked with per-chunk fingerprints, but stable identity must be anchor-first. Existing memory_index child-row schema and chunk embedding cache are close; current chunk_index/chunk_label identity is insufficient for heading renames, reorders, and split/merge operations.

claim: CocoIndex code_embedding chunks every file with RecursiveSplitter, embeds each chunk independently, and stores line ranges, but the example's durable key is generated from chunk text through IdGenerator rather than an explicit semantic chunk identity.
evidence: main.py:89-107 splits file text and maps chunks
main.py:69-85 embeds/stores each chunk with start_line and end_line
id.py:89-99 defines IdGenerator for distinct IDs even with repeated chunk content
id.py:126-149 generates stable sequence IDs from dep fingerprint plus ordinal

claim: CocoIndex ships separator and recursive splitters that return position-aware chunks, but the Chunk resource itself has no chunk_id, heading path, AST path, or fingerprint field.
evidence: text.py:39-84 defines SeparatorSplitter returning chunks with position information
text.py:120-180 defines RecursiveSplitter with syntax awareness and overlap
text.py:183-209 converts raw chunks into text plus byte/char/line/column positions
chunk.py:25-36 defines Chunk as text/start/end only

claim: The normal memory parser and save embedding path still operate at whole-document granularity.
evidence: memory-parser.ts:342-385 returns one ParsedMemory with full content and one contentHash
embedding-pipeline.ts:119-155 computes one cache key from parsed.content and generates one weighted document embedding

claim: The repo already has chunked indexing for large full-auto saves with independent child embeddings and per-chunk embedding cache, but it is not the general spec-section incremental model K1.4 needs.
evidence: memory-save.ts:2190-2217 computes shouldChunkContent but still generates whole-doc embedding first
memory-save.ts:2339-2348 calls indexChunkedMemoryFile only when shouldChunkContent is true
chunking-orchestrator.ts:121-126 requires full-auto planner mode plus needsChunking
anchor-chunker.ts:60-62 and 303-307 set chunking threshold above 50000 chars

claim: memory_index can already represent multiple child rows per document, but current chunk identity columns are order/label oriented and lack chunk_fingerprint.
evidence: vector-index-schema.ts:2412-2414 defines parent_id, chunk_index, and chunk_label
vector-index-schema.ts:863-898 added parent/chunk migration and indexes
chunking-orchestrator.ts:326-337 writes parent_id, chunk_index, chunk_label, and parent content_hash to chunk rows

claim: Anchor IDs are the best stable chunk identity for spec docs; heading slugs and fixed positions should be fallback only.
evidence: anchor-chunker.ts:70-118 extracts paired ANCHOR sections
anchor-chunker.ts:153-176 labels anchor chunks by anchor id
anchor-chunker.ts:288-295 only uses anchor strategy when paired anchors produce at least two chunks
spec-doc survey found common opening ANCHOR markers before H2 sections and heading labels that may be renamed or renumbered

claim: Chunk-level embedding does not require chunk-only UX because hybrid search already has a chunk-to-parent aggregation stage.
evidence: hybrid-search.ts:1358-1368 detects chunk results by parentMemoryId and chunkIndex
hybrid-search.ts:1372-1388 collapses chunks into parent results with _chunkHits metadata
